# Contract: Reconciliation FFI (UniFFI Swift boundary)

**Feature**: `012-cc-reconciliation` | **Date**: 2026-07-19
**Module**: `kaname-core` (Rust) → `KanameCore` (Swift, generated by UniFFI)

Additive to the P1 + all-reader + balance-chain `engine-ffi` contracts. The "external interface" is the
exported UniFFI surface (not an HTTP API); generated Swift signatures are the consumer-facing form. This
slice adds **one function** (`reconcile_statement`), **two new types** (`ReconcileStatus` enum +
`ReconcileResult` record), and **two additive fields** on the reused `ParsedStatement` record
(`printedTotalDebits` / `printedTotalCredits`). It **reuses** the existing `Decimal`/`NaiveDate` custom
types and the `ParsedTransaction`/`Direction` types **unchanged**, and requires **no `uniffi.toml`
change**. Mirrors the `check_balance_chain` precedent exactly.

---

## Exported function

### `reconcile_statement`

| | |
|-|-|
| Rust | `#[uniffi::export] pub fn reconcile_statement(statement: ParsedStatement) -> ReconcileResult` |
| Swift | `public func reconcileStatement(statement: ParsedStatement) -> ReconcileResult` |

**Contract**
- Pure verification over an **already-parsed** `ParsedStatement` (produced by any `read_*_statement`).
  **Never** opens a file/PDF, never touches the network/clock/locale/global state (FR-018/020/022).
- **Three-tier ladder** (FR-005..FR-009, D6/D7/D8), `tolerance = 1.00` inclusive:
  - **Primary** — if `printedTotalDebits` **or** `printedTotalCredits` is present: compare each present
    total to the corresponding read sum with `(read − printed).abs() <= 1.00`; `status =
    .reconciled` iff **every present** total passes, else `.needsReview`. Echoes `printedDebits` /
    `printedCredits`; leaves the fallback fields + `reason` `nil`.
  - **Fallback** — else if **both** `printedOpeningBalance` **and** `printedClosingBalance` are present:
    `expected = closing − opening`, `computed = readDebits − readCredits`; `status = .reconciled` iff
    `(computed − expected).abs() <= 1.00`, else `.needsReview`. Echoes `expectedBalanceChange` /
    `computedBalanceChange`.
  - **Neutral** — else: `status == nil` (**not** `.needsReview`) and `reason == "no printed totals
    extracted"` (FR-004, D2). One-or-neither printed balance ⇒ neutral (the fallback needs both).
- **`readDebits`/`readCredits` are ALWAYS set** — Σ of each row's `amount` by its own `direction`
  marker; empty statement ⇒ `0.00`/`0.00`; exact `Decimal` (FR-002, SC-012). Rows are **never** dropped,
  mutated, or reordered (FR-003).
- **Precedence**: any printed total present routes to the primary path; the fallback is **never**
  consulted in that case, even when both balances are also present (FR-005, SC-008).
- **Purity / totality**: identical input ⇒ identical output; never panics (FR-018, SC-013). No failure is
  surfaced to Swift (total function).

**Consumer expectations (app/test)**
- Treat the three outcomes distinctly: `.reconciled` (verified), `.needsReview` (figures disagree beyond
  ₹1.00 — an **advisory**, rows still valid), and `status == nil` (**neutral** — insufficient printed
  anchors to check; **not** a failure). Never coerce neutral into `.needsReview` (US3, SC-006).
- Read the audit detail for display/logging: primary → `readDebits`/`readCredits` +
  `printedDebits`/`printedCredits`; fallback → `expectedBalanceChange`/`computedBalanceChange`; neutral →
  `reason`. Compare money as `Foundation.Decimal` (never float).

> Internally: `reconcile_statement` wraps `reconcile(&statement)` (`statement/reconcile.rs`), exactly as
> `check_balance_chain` wraps `check(&statement)`. `lib.rs` re-exports the function (`pub use ffi::{…}`)
> and the two types (`pub use statement::reconcile::{ReconcileResult, ReconcileStatus};`);
> `statement/mod.rs` gains `pub mod reconcile;`.

---

## Types

### `ReconcileStatus` (NEW) — `uniffi::Enum`

| Rust | Swift |
|-|-|
| `enum ReconcileStatus { Reconciled, NeedsReview }` | `.reconciled` / `.needsReview` |

Two variants only. The **neutral** outcome is **not** a variant — it is `ReconcileResult.status == nil`
(`Option<ReconcileStatus>` → Swift `ReconcileStatus?`). Mirrors `ChainStatus`
(`Debug/Clone/Copy/PartialEq/Eq/uniffi::Enum`).

### `ReconcileResult` (NEW) — `uniffi::Record`

Swift shape (generated):
```swift
public struct ReconcileResult {
    public var status: ReconcileStatus?          // nil == neutral
    public var readDebits: Decimal               // Foundation.Decimal (always set)
    public var readCredits: Decimal              // always set
    public var printedDebits: Decimal?           // primary path
    public var printedCredits: Decimal?          // primary path
    public var expectedBalanceChange: Decimal?   // fallback path
    public var computedBalanceChange: Decimal?   // fallback path
    public var reason: String?                   // neutral path
}
```
(`Decimal` here is the custom-type alias that surfaces as `Foundation.Decimal` in app code.) Mirrors
`ChainResult` (`Debug/Clone/PartialEq/uniffi::Record`; no `Copy`/`Eq` — holds `Decimal`/`String`).

### `ParsedStatement` (REUSED — two additive fields)

`record ParsedStatement` gains `printedTotalDebits: String?` and `printedTotalCredits: String?` (from the
two new `Option<Decimal>` Rust fields). All prior fields are **unchanged**; readers other than Yes/IOB
leave the two new fields `nil`. Updated Swift shape (for reference):

> `ParsedStatement { bankCode: String; lines: [ParsedTransaction]; erroredLines: [String]; periodStart:
> String?; periodEnd: String?; cardLast4: String?; printedOpeningBalance: String?; printedClosingBalance:
> String?; printedTotalDebits: String?; printedTotalCredits: String?; confidence: Double }`.

> **`printedTotalSpend` is intentionally absent** — the web model carries it but reconciliation never
> reads it, so it is not added (FR-011, D9).

### Reused unchanged

- `record ParsedTransaction`; `enum Direction { Debit, Credit }` → `.debit`/`.credit`.
- Custom types `Decimal`↔`String`↔`Foundation.Decimal` and `NaiveDate`↔ISO-8601 `String`
  (P1, `ffi.rs` + `uniffi.toml`). Money never crosses as `f64`.

---

## Contract tests (test-first — FR-024/027)

**Rust (`cargo test`)** — engine-side truth (`tests/parity.rs` + `reconcile.rs` unit tests):
- `yes_statement_reconciles`: read the extended Yes fixture → `reconcile_statement(statement)` yields
  `status == Some(ReconcileStatus::Reconciled)`, `read_debits 100.00`, `read_credits 9000.00`,
  `printed_debits Some(100.00)`, `printed_credits Some(9000.00)` (SC-002/014).
- `iob_statement_reconciles`: read the IOB fixture → `status == Some(Reconciled)`, `read_debits 3500.00`,
  `read_credits 1000.00`, printed totals echoed and matching (SC-003/014).
- `statement_without_printed_totals_is_neutral`: read `icici/credit_card/basic.json` → `status == None`
  **and** `reason == Some("no printed totals extracted")` (SC-006).
- `reconcile.rs` unit tests additionally cover: debit-mismatch → `NeedsReview` (with read/printed
  detail); 0.50-within-tolerance → `Reconciled`; exactly-1.00 boundary → `Reconciled`;
  only-one-total-present; both-present-one-mismatch → `NeedsReview`; balance-change fallback →
  `Reconciled`; primary-takes-precedence-over-fallback; only-one-balance → neutral; empty-rows sums
  `0.00` (FR-023, SC-004/005/007/008).

**Swift (Swift Testing, `import KanameCore`, `ios/Tests/ReconcileTests.swift`)** — "core ↔ Swift
reconcile" (mirrors `ICICIBankParseTests.balanceChainReconciles`):
- Read the extended Yes statement via `readYesStatement(...)`, then `reconcileStatement(statement:)` →
  `result.status == .reconciled`, with `printedDebits`/`printedCredits` surfaced (`Decimal(string:)`
  value-equality).
- Read the IOB statement → `reconcileStatement` → `.status == .reconciled`.
- Read the ICICI statement (no printed totals) → `reconcileStatement` → `.status == nil` (neutral).
- `status` surfaces as `ReconcileStatus?`; assert `nil` for neutral and `.reconciled` otherwise. Any
  comment sits on its **own line above** the code (swift-format `[Spacing]` forbids trailing inline
  `//`).

---

## Stability / compatibility
- Adding one function + two types is additive; the two new `ParsedStatement` fields are additive
  (defaulted `nil`), so existing callers and fixtures are unaffected.
- The generated `Generated/kaname_core.swift` + `KanameCoreFFI.xcframework` are build artifacts, rebuilt
  by `make core-xcframework` (which runs **before** `tuist generate`). **This contract**, not the
  generated files, is the reviewed source of truth.
